current_deliverable: RAAI domain confirmation series — DC#1 complete (DPO, C70). Advancing toward D4 through domain confirmation accumulation across training paradigm types.
progress: C70 complete. DC#1: DPO confirmed as RAAI instantiation. Two derivations + one DC + ZR cycle 2. RAAI-DPI shown to close DPO escape route — the training feedback type (OA-type) is the information bottleneck, not the RL architecture. DPO is a paradigmatically distinct training approach (supervised contrastive loss, no reward model, no PPO) — RAAI surviving this boundary crossing validates the scope claim. Decision document: raai-dpo-domain-confirmation-dc1.md. Passive: RAAI ZR cycle 2 (C68-C70), CIAP ZR cycle 4, SI #44 AAA ZR cycle 4, AIIP ZR cycle 18, AATE ZR cycle 15. D4 streak 62.
remaining: C71 PRIORITY A: RAAI DC#2 — Constitutional AI. ConstitutionalAI uses structured critique prompts (CPP > 0 in principle). Key question: does CPP > 0 in ConstitutionalAI reduce the DPI-derived AAA bound? Test: is the AI critique model OA-calibrated (if yes, same DPI applies, RAAI holds)? Or is the critique genuinely CA-calibrated (if yes, partial R2 escape)? This is the most theoretically rich DC because it tests the R2 prescription boundary.

C71 PRIORITY B: RAAI DC#3 — RLAIF. AI-generated feedback where the AI rater is itself OA-calibrated (RLHF or DPO trained). If AI rater has AAA (by RAAI applied to rater), then RLAIF feedback is OA-calibrated → same DPI bound on student model → RAAI cascade. Interesting: RLAIF-as-RAAI-cascade is a novel structural result.
C71 PRIORITY C: RAAI DC#4 — SFT on human ratings. Supervised fine-tuning using human quality ratings as training signal. Simplest A(x)-feedback case. Confirms RAAI at baseline.
C71 PRIORITY D: CIAP DC#3 — economic/market competitive gaming. Third ontological type for SI #45.
Long-horizon: K5 Test 3 execution (blocked — external LLM API); EP2/Test 2 MVE (training-level access); EP6 A'-deterioration test (checkpoint access)
